event: Allegory of March — Triumph of Minerva (Detail from Palazzo Schifanoia)
symbols: builders on scaffolding — the constructive activities of springtime renewal
nobles on horseback — the aristocratic life governed by the seasonal cycle
white horse — nobility and status in the courtly procession
description: A fresco detail showing two registers of activity. In the upper portion, workers build a structure on scaffolding, with horses and riders nearby in a landscape with buildings. In the lower portion, richly dressed young courtiers ride horses and stand in a procession, wearing elaborate Renaissance clothing in bright colors. A greyhound runs alongside. The overall composition is dense with activity, depicting both labor and leisure in a seasonal cycle. The palette is rich with reds, whites, blues, and golds against a blue sky.

_meta.title: Allegory of March – Triumph of Minerva and Sign of Aries. Frescos in Palazzo Schifanoia (detail)
